bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no prior opponent offers or messages, I should open with my maximum-utility bid and avoid conceding before learning the opponent’s priorities. This targets full utility by selecting my top value, valueC, on every issue, especially the highest-weight issueF and the medium-weight issues C/D/E.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02, and there is no need to concede much yet. The opponent’s all-valueA offer is very poor for my preferences, and their claim that they cannot move is cheap talk unsupported by any concession history. I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding the high-importance issues at valueC.

It is still very early (t=0.04), so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent has repeated an all-valueA package and claims it is fixed, but that is cheap talk and gives no evidence of real concession yet, so I will hold firm on the high-importance issues C, D, E, and especially F.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on the two lowest-weight issues. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA offer and used firm-language messages, but their bidding behavior shows no real concession yet, so I should not make a large move toward their position.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.08) and the opponent has not shown any numeric concession, repeatedly offering the same all-valueA package despite claims of firmness. To avoid simply repeating my last bid while making a small concession on low-weight issues, I am moving issueA down to valueA and keeping issueB at valueB, while holding valueC on the higher-importance issues C, D, E, and F.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 by conceding only the lowest-weight issues while holding firm on the more important issues C, D, E, and especially F. At t=0.10 it is still early, so there is no need to match the opponent’s low-value package; their messages claim firmness, but the actual offers show a small concession on issueD, so I treat the messages as cheap talk.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.86 for me, since it is still early at t=0.12 and there is no need to concede toward the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s messages claim a firm mandate, but their actual offers show slight movement on issueD, so I will reciprocate there while holding my most important issueF and other high-weight issues C/E at valueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78, since it is early at t=0.14 but the opponent has held nearly firm and only moved on issueD. I treat their authorization claims as cheap talk; the actual offers show a small concession on issueD, so I keep my highest-weight issueF and issueE at valueC while making an additional concession on issueC to valueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around the early-game concession range, keeping my most important issueF and other high-value issues strong while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB. The opponent’s messages claim hard limits, but their actual offers have moved on issueD and issueF, so I treat the claims as cheap talk and respond with a valid counter that acknowledges some movement without over-conceding at t=0.16.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the high-mid range by holding firm on my most important issues, especially issueF and issueE, while conceding on lower-weight issues and offering compromise values where the opponent has shown movement. Time is still early at t=0.18, so I should not accept their very low-utility package or over-concede despite repeated “authorization” claims. The opponent has only slightly moved on issueD and issueF, so I am using issueD as a compromise while preserving higher-value positions on my key issues.

At t=0.20 there is still time, so I am targeting a solid positive utility while making a visible concession from my last position rather than accepting the opponent’s very low-value package. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim hard limits, but the offer history contradicts that by showing small movements on issueD, issueF, and now issueC, so I trust the bids over the claims. I am conceding on lower-weight issueA/issueB and partially on issueC/issueD, while holding issueE and issueF where my utility impact is highest.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 by keeping my highest-weight issues issueF and issueE at valueC while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB to valueA. The opponent’s offers and messages claim firmness, but the actual bids show small movement on issueC, issueD, and issueF, so at t=0.22 I will use a tradeoff that incorporates their signaled concessions on issueC/issueD without giving up my key issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility by holding firm on my highest-value issues, especially issueF and issueE, while keeping concessions on the lower-weight issues issueA and issueB. Time is still early-mid at t=0.24, so I do not need to accept the opponent’s very low offers; their messages about “authorization limits” are contradicted by their actual movement on issueC, issueD, and issueF, so I treat them as cheap talk. Since the opponent most recently offered issueD: valueC, I can include that while maintaining valueC on my key issues and offering valueB on issueC as a moderate bridge.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the high 0.7s because it is only t=0.26, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim hard limits, but their actual offers have moved on issueD, issueC, issueE, and issueF, so I am trusting the bid history rather than the cheap-talk constraints. I am conceding on lower-weight issueA/issueB and partly on issueC/issueE while holding firm on the highest-weight issueF and preserving value on issueD where they have already shown movement.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.70 by holding issueF at valueC, since it is my highest-weight issue and the opponent has now signaled willingness there. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are contradicted by their actual concessions over time, so I am trusting the offer history: they have separately shown movement on issueD, issueE, and issueF, and I am combining those while conceding issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong mid-game utility around 0.74 by holding firm on the high-importance issueF and issueD where the opponent has already shown some willingness to move, while conceding on lower-value areas. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim a hard limit, but their bids contradict that by changing issueD, issueE, issueF, issueC, and now issueA, so I trust the bidding pattern rather than the claims. At t=0.30 there is no need to accept their low-utility package, but I will avoid repeating my last bid and incorporate their latest movement on issueA.

At t=0.32 there is still time, so I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid around 0.78, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and some mid-weight issues while holding firm on high-value issueF. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim final authority, but their offers have in fact moved across issueC, issueD, issueE, issueF, and issueA, so I trust the bidding behavior over the cheap talk and make a non-repeated counter that incorporates some of their signaled concessions.

I am targeting a still-positive mid-game utility around 0.70 by holding firm on the most important issueF at valueC and keeping issueD at valueC, which the opponent has repeatedly shown they can offer. Since time is only 0.34, I do not need to drop to their latest low-utility package, but I am conceding issueC to valueA and keeping low-weight issues A/B at valueA to move closer to their pattern. Their repeated “final authorization” messages are not credible because their actual offers have continued to change.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility by conceding my low-weight issues A and B to valueA, while holding the most important issueF at valueC and using the opponent’s latest movement to issueC: valueC. The opponent’s repeated “final authorization” messages are contradicted by their actual offer changes, so I treat them as cheap talk; at t=0.36 there is still time to seek a better package while making a meaningful concession on D and E.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.784, which is a meaningful concession from my maximum while still protecting the high-importance issueF. At t=0.38 there is some mid-game pressure to move, but not enough to accept the opponent’s much lower-utility packages; their repeated “limit” messages are contradicted by actual concessions, especially on issueC and issueD. I am conceding low-weight issueA/issueB and offering issueC/issueE at valueB while keeping issueD and issueF strong.

I am targeting a moderate-utility bid around the mid-game, conceding further from my last offer while still protecting my highest-weight issueF at valueC. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim a hard limit, but their actual offers have continued to move across issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and respond to the numerical concessions. Since time is 0.40, I concede on lower/mid-weight issues and reduce issueD to valueB to avoid repeating my last bid exactly.

I targeted a solid mid-game utility by keeping issueF at valueC, since it is my highest-weight issue, while aligning with the opponent’s latest concessions on issueD and issueE. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are not credible because their offers have continued to change, and their latest bid is their best so far for me, showing gradual concession. At t=0.42 there is still time, so I am not dropping to their exact offer yet, but I am using their signaled flexibility to propose a plausible tradeoff.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.78, conceding on low-weight issueA and issueB and partially on issueD/issueE while holding firm on high-weight issueF. The opponent’s repeated “final authorization” messages are contradicted by their actual concessions, and their latest offer signals some willingness on issueC and issueE, so I incorporate those while avoiding their very low issueF/issueD demand.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility while still incorporating concessions on low-weight issueA and issueB and accepting the opponent’s recent movement on issueD and issueE. At t=0.46 there is still time, so I should not drop to their last offer; their messages repeatedly claim limits, but the offer history shows they keep changing and conceding across issues. I am holding firm on issueF because it is my highest-weight issue, while using values they have already signaled on other issues.

I targeted a moderate-utility compromise while still protecting issueF, my highest-weight issue, at valueC. At t=0.48 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest package wholesale; their offers show real movement despite repeated “authorization limit” messages, so I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and asking for valueB on issueE.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.74, preserving my highest-weight issueF at valueC while conceding lower-weight issueA and much of issueC. The opponent’s repeated “no further movement” messages are contradicted by their changing offers, and their latest bid shows willingness on issueB, issueD, and issueE, while prior offers show they can move to issueF:valueC.

I am targeting a moderate-utility bid around the midgame, keeping issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue while conceding several lower or recently contested issues. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim final authority, but their actual offers have continued to move, including recent willingness on issueF and earlier willingness on issueD, so I am countering close to their last bid while asking for issueD at valueB.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66 by holding issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue, while accepting several valueB/valueA concessions elsewhere. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim final authority limits, but their actual offers have moved over time, so I treat those statements as cheap talk and respond to the concrete latest package by keeping their recent concessions on issueA, issueD, and issueE while improving issueF for me.

I am targeting a moderate improvement over the opponent’s latest offer by keeping the high-importance issueF and issueE at valueC while asking for a small concession on issueD to valueB. At t=0.56 there is still room to bargain, and the opponent’s repeated “final authorization” messages are not credible because their offers have continued to improve over time.

I am targeting a utility around the high-0.78 range, which is still strong for me while making visible concessions on issueC and issueD. At t=0.58 there is some time pressure, but not enough to give up heavily weighted issueF or issueE; the opponent’s repeated “limit” messages are contradicted by their actual concessions, so I rely on the bid history instead.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.78 by preserving my highest-weight issueF at valueC and keeping issueC at valueC, while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB to valueA. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim limits, but their offers have steadily varied and recently included issueC and issueF at valueC, so I treat the latest offer as meaningful movement and ask only for a modest improvement on issueD.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.825 while making a modest concession from my last bid by matching the opponent’s latest movement on issueA. At t=0.62 there is some time pressure, but not enough to give up high-weight issueF, especially since the opponent has previously offered issueF:valueC and issueC:valueC; their repeated “final authority” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their actual concessions.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.82 while avoiding repeating my prior bid, holding firm on issueF because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim “final authorization,” but their actual offers have moved over time, including prior willingness to put issueF at valueC, so I am treating those claims as cheap talk. At t=0.64 I can concede on lower-value/less favorable areas like issueA and issueE while asking them to preserve the recent strong movement on issueB, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.78 by holding firm on the high-value issueF and one important issueC while accepting the opponent’s recent concessions on issueB and issueE and conceding issueD. At t=0.66 there is some time pressure, but not enough to accept their latest lower-utility package outright; their repeated “limit” messages are contradicted by ongoing bid movement, so I rely on the offer history instead.

At t=0.68 I am targeting a solid positive utility while making a focused concession pattern: I keep issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue, and I largely mirror the opponent’s latest acceptable-looking structure while asking only for issueE to improve to valueB. The opponent’s repeated “final authority” messages are not credible because their offers have continued to change and improve over time, so I rely on the bid history rather than the wording.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around the high-0.7 range, since at t=0.70 there is real time pressure but still room to avoid collapsing to the opponent’s last low-value package. The opponent’s offers have gradually improved on some issues but remain inconsistent, and their repeated “final/authorized” messages are contradicted by their actual concessions, so I am treating the numbers rather than the claims as informative. I keep issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue, while conceding broadly to valueB on the remaining issues to move toward their latest package.

At t=0.72 I am conceding toward the opponent’s latest structure while still protecting high-weight issueF and issueE; this bid targets a strong but realistic utility by keeping issueF=valueC and restoring issueE to valueB. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim hard limits, but their offers have steadily improved across several issues, so I rely on the offer history rather than the cheap-talk constraints.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.82 while making a late-stage concession on issueE and issueB to improve acceptability before the deadline. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk contradicted by their changing bids, but their latest offer signals willingness on issueA, issueC, and issueD; I preserve my most important issueF at valueC, which they have offered multiple times before.

I am targeting a weighted utility of about 0.743, which is a meaningful concession but still preserves my highest-priority issueF at valueC and issueC at valueC. At t=0.76, time pressure makes it prudent to move toward a package the opponent has already signaled willingness to offer, while ignoring their repeated “final authorization” messages as cheap talk. Their bids show intermittent concessions on issueF, issueC, and issueD, so I am proposing a prior opponent-backed structure rather than pushing my last higher-demand bid.
